description: Add golden-file integration tests for the five remaining commands without golden-file coverage: treb verify, treb sync, treb prune, treb reset, and treb migrate. Fix any output format discrepancies found during golden file creation.

userStories.title: Prune Command Golden File Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden-file integration tests for treb prune so that dry-run candidate display, destructive removal with backup, chain ID filtering, --include-pending, JSON output, and clean-registry paths are locked down against regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: integration_prune.rs contains 7 #[test] functions (prune_dry_run, prune_destructive, prune_nothing, prune_chain_filter, prune_include_pending, prune_json_dry_run, prune_uninitialized)
prune_dry_run golden output contains a table with ID, Kind, Reason, Chain ID columns and the candidate count summary
prune_destructive golden output contains removal summary and backup path message
prune_nothing golden output contains 'Nothing to prune.'
prune_chain_filter golden output only contains chain-1 entries (no chain-42220 entries)
prune_include_pending golden output contains an OrphanedPendingEntry row
prune_json_dry_run golden output is valid JSON with id, kind, reason, chainId fields
Backup paths in golden output are normalized (epoch timestamps replaced with <EPOCH>)
PathNormalizer replaces workdir paths with <PROJECT_ROOT>
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings

userStories.title: Reset Command Golden File Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden-file integration tests for treb reset so that full reset with backup, network-scoped reset, namespace-scoped reset, empty registry, and JSON output paths are locked down against regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: integration_reset.rs contains 6 #[test] functions (reset_full, reset_network_filter, reset_namespace_filter, reset_empty, reset_json, reset_uninitialized)
reset_full golden output contains 'Reset complete.' with 4 per-type removal count lines and a backup path
reset_network_filter golden output shows only chain-1 removal counts
reset_namespace_filter golden output shows only namespace-'staging' removal counts
reset_empty golden output contains 'Nothing to reset.'
reset_json golden output is valid JSON with removed_deployments, removed_transactions, removed_safe_transactions, removed_governor_proposals, backup_path fields
Backup paths are normalized (epoch timestamps replaced with <EPOCH>)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings

userStories.title: Migrate Command Golden File Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden-file integration tests for treb migrate config and treb migrate registry so that config migration (v1→v2 dry-run, v1→v2 with backup, already-v2 detection) and registry migration (up-to-date, dry-run) paths are locked down against regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: integration_migrate.rs contains 8 #[test] functions (migrate_config_dry_run_v1, migrate_config_v1_to_v2, migrate_config_already_v2, migrate_config_json_already_v2, migrate_config_json_dry_run, migrate_config_no_treb_toml, migrate_registry_up_to_date, migrate_registry_dry_run_up_to_date)
migrate_config_dry_run_v1 golden output contains v2 TOML with [accounts.deployer] section
migrate_config_v1_to_v2 golden output contains 'Migration complete.' and backup path, plus treb.toml artifact showing v2 content
migrate_config_already_v2 golden output contains 'already v2 format'
Both JSON tests produce valid JSON with correct schema
migrate_config_no_treb_toml uses expect_err(true) and captures error message
migrate_registry_up_to_date golden output contains 'up to date'
Backup file paths are normalized
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings

userStories.title: Verify Command Golden File Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden-file integration tests for treb verify covering status-based paths so that already-verified skip, no unverified deployments, unknown verifier error, uninitialized project error, and JSON output are locked down against regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: integration_verify.rs contains 6 #[test] functions (verify_already_verified, verify_all_none_unverified, verify_json_already_verified, verify_error_unknown_verifier, verify_uninitialized, verify_no_foundry_project)
verify_already_verified golden output contains 'already verified'
verify_all_none_unverified golden output contains 'No unverified deployments found.'
verify_json_already_verified golden output is valid JSON with correct camelCase field names
verify_error_unknown_verifier uses expect_err(true) and captures error with valid verifier list
Both uninitialized/no-project tests use expect_err(true)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings

userStories.title: Sync Command Golden File Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden-file integration tests for treb sync covering empty-state paths so that no safe transactions, network filter with no matches, uninitialized project, and JSON output are locked down against regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: integration_sync.rs contains 5 #[test] functions (sync_no_safe_txs, sync_no_safe_txs_network_filter, sync_json_empty, sync_uninitialized, sync_no_foundry_project)
sync_no_safe_txs golden output contains 'No safe transactions in the registry.'
sync_no_safe_txs_network_filter golden output contains 'No safe transactions found for network 1.'
sync_json_empty golden output is valid JSON with zero counts and empty errors array
Both error tests use expect_err(true) with appropriate error messages
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings

userStories.title: Output Format Fixes and Final Cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all golden files from P9-US-001 through P9-US-005 reviewed for output correctness against the Go CLI so that output formatting is consistent and the EpochNormalizer is properly shared across test files.
acceptanceCriteria: All golden files from P9-US-001 through P9-US-005 have been reviewed for output correctness
Prune table format (columns, alignment, summary) matches Go CLI output
Reset summary format (per-type counts, backup message) matches Go CLI output
Migrate messages (completion, backup, already-v2, registry up-to-date) match Go CLI output
Verify status messages (already verified, no unverified) match Go CLI output
Sync empty-state messages match Go CLI output
JSON output field naming is consistent with Go CLI for all 5 commands
EpochNormalizer is available in framework/normalizer.rs if multiple test files need it
All golden files are regenerated after fixes
cargo test --workspace passes (all phases 5-9)
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings
